summary: Backend-only testing of push notification implementation. All 19 tests passed for notification endpoints, message notification triggers, activity invite notifications, and RSVP notifications.

POST_notifications_register.details: Endpoint registers Expo push tokens for authenticated users. Supports both iOS and Android platforms. Stores push_token, push_platform, and push_token_updated_at in user document.

DELETE_notifications_unregister.details: Endpoint removes push token and platform from user document. Requires authentication.

message_notification_trigger.details: When user sends a message via POST /api/messages, an async task sends push notification to recipient with channel 'messages'. Notification includes sender name, truncated message text (max 100 chars), and navigation data.

activity_invite_notification.details: When activity is created with invites, notify_users() is called for all invited user IDs (excluding creator) with channel 'activities'. Notification includes activity title, creator name, and activity date.

activity_rsvp_notification.details: When user RSVPs to an activity, creator receives notification (unless RSVPing own activity). Notification uses 'activities' channel with status-specific message.

notification_channels_android.details: Frontend NotificationContext.tsx sets up three Android notification channels: 'default' (MAX importance), 'messages' (HIGH importance), 'activities' (DEFAULT importance). All with #4c6fff light color.

NotificationProvider_integration.details: NotificationProvider wraps the app in _layout.tsx inside AuthProvider. Automatically registers for push notifications when user logs in via useEffect on sessionToken/user.

expo_push_token_validation.details: send_expo_push_notification() validates tokens start with 'ExponentPushToken' before sending. Invalid tokens are silently skipped.

critical_code_review_comments: Push notifications are correctly implemented using Expo Push Notifications service
Notifications are sent asynchronously via asyncio.create_task() to avoid blocking API responses
Frontend automatically registers push token when user authenticates
All three notification types (messages, activity invites, RSVPs) properly trigger notifications with appropriate channels

test_credentials.dynamically_created: Each test creates unique users with prefix TEST_notif_

seed_data_creation: Dynamic user creation per test for isolation
context_for_next_testing_agent: Push notification backend implementation is fully functional. Backend endpoints /api/notifications/register and /api/notifications/unregister work correctly. Notification triggers are in place for: 1) Messages (channel: messages), 2) Activity invites (channel: activities), 3) Activity RSVPs (channel: activities). Frontend NotificationContext is properly integrated in _layout.tsx and sets up Android channels. To fully test on real devices, use Expo Go app with physical iOS/Android device.

implementation_notes.backend_notification_flow: 1. User registers token via POST /api/notifications/register → 2. Token stored in users collection → 3. When notification-triggering action occurs, notify_user() or notify_users() is called → 4. send_expo_push_notification() sends to Expo Push API
